# datahub_glossary_node (Data Source)

**DataHub ✅ | DataHub Cloud ✅**

Looks up an existing DataHub glossary node (Term Group) by `node_id`.

Use this to reference a term group that already exists in DataHub -- for example one created in the DataHub UI or via the Python SDK -- as a `parent_node` in a `datahub_glossary_node` or `datahub_glossary_term` resource without taking ownership of it.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "datahub_glossary_node" "finance" {
  node_id = "finance"
}

output "finance_urn" {
  value = data.datahub_glossary_node.finance.urn
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `node_id` (String) The unique identifier of the glossary node to look up (the URN suffix, e.g. `finance`).

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) Description of the term group. Empty if not set.
- `name` (String) Human-readable display name of the term group.
- `parent_node` (String) Full URN of the parent glossary node, or empty if this is a root-level term group.
- `urn` (String) Full DataHub URN for this glossary node (e.g. `urn:li:glossaryNode:finance`).
